title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston named most collision-prone city in U.S., with drivers averaging 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, Massachusetts, also rank in bottom five for collision frequency.
Providence, Rhode Island, ranked 194th, among the 10 riskiest cities for drivers in Allstate's report.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, led a 105 mph wrong-way chase on the Bourne Bridge before barricading himself at home.
Deleon faces charges including speeding, obstructing emergency vehicles, and assault after the high-speed pursuit.
A judge ordered Deleon held without bail; his next court date is set for August 5 in Falmouth District Court.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police seek help identifying a suspect in the armed robbery of a lemonade stand on West Ninth Street.
Surveillance images show a suspect wearing black pants, a black hat, and carrying a black backpack.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested for the June 10 robbery involving a weapon and theft from a lemonade stand.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James considers future NBA teams, with Boston Celtics mentioned by Rich Paul as a potential landing spot.
Celtics duo Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens recognized by Rich Paul, though not top contenders for James.
Boston adds Mitchell Robinson and Mike Conley Jr. as part of offseason roster retooling by Brad Stevens.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticizes Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the 76ers, comparing it to the Mavericks trading Luka Doncic.
Parish believes Red Auerbach would be displeased with the trade, feeling it would have 'made him nauseated.'
Despite disapproval, Parish hopes the Celtics and Brown maintain a good relationship and suggests his jersey be retired.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum bid farewell to Jaylen Brown on social media after Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers.
The Celtics traded Brown for Paul George and draft picks; reports suggest Brown did not request the trade.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics during the trade process and will face Boston four times next season.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics sign Neemias Queta to a four-year, $56 million extension through the 2030-31 season.
Queta's 2026-27 team option was picked up, adding four guaranteed years to his contract.
Queta averaged 10.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 1.3 blocks in 76 games during the 2025-26 season.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
The restaurant's historic building, likely built in 1716, housed a Revolutionary War-era newspaper.
Union Oyster House serves 1,500 guests daily, with clam chowder and oysters as popular menu items.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's summer events include the Boston Pops Fireworks on July 4, hosted by Jane Lynch.
Gillette Stadium will host a World Cup quarterfinal on July 9, affecting local traffic patterns.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail begins on July 11, featuring over 60 tall ships in Boston Harbor.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston has hosted six U.S. presidents, including John F. Kennedy and Barack Obama.
In 2003, artist Stan Kotzen created a painting of over 50 celebrities who dined at the restaurant.
Famous guests include actors Matt Damon and Meryl Streep, and athletes Muhammad Ali and Tiger Woods.
